steps.title: Turn the g table into an f table
steps.content:
The table gives $g(x)$, but the question asks for the y-intercept of $y=f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\dfrac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each table output by $x+3$ to get $f(x)$:
  • $f(-21)=2(-21+3)=-36$
  • $f(-9)=0(-9+3)=0$
  • $f(15)=4(15+3)=72$

steps.title: Use Desmos regression on the derived points
steps.content: Enter the derived $f$ points as $x_1=[-21,-9,15]$ and $y_1=[-36,0,72]$. Then type $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos returns $m=3$ and $b=27$, so $f(x)=3x+27$.

steps.title: Identify the intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$. From $f(x)=3x+27$, the intercept is $(0,27)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
